genetic_lineage.overview: Cortese is indigenous to Piedmont with no confirmed parentage. DNA studies confirm it is distinct from other Italian white varieties.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Indigenous to southeastern Piedmont.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Cortese is one of Piedmont's few indigenous white varieties of quality, alongside Arneis and Favorita. Its genetic isolation confirms ancient local cultivation.

clones.name: Clone CVT 3
clones.characteristics: Standard certified clone with good acidity and mineral expression.

clones.name: Gavi Massal Selection
clones.characteristics: Traditional selections from historic Gavi vineyards. Maximum finesse and aging potential.

clones.name: Rovereto Selection
clones.characteristics: Selections from the prestigious Rovereto commune. Structured wines with longevity.

terroir.effect: Classic Gavi soil. Produces wines with pronounced minerality and elegance.
Common in Gavi. Provides good water retention and mineral complexity.
Lighter soils producing more aromatic, earlier-drinking wines.

susceptibilities.notes: Can be affected in humid conditions.

resistances.notes: Can maintain quality at moderate yields, unlike some varieties.

disease_profile.general_notes: Cortese is a late-ripening variety that produces crisp, mineral wines. The variety benefits from well-drained hillside sites with temperature variation. Excessive yields dilute quality significantly. The best wines require aging to reveal full complexity.

regional_expressions.style_summary: Piedmont's most celebrated white wine, producing crisp, mineral wines from the town of Gavi and surrounding communes. Quality ranges from simple to age-worthy depending on site and producer. The best examples rival fine white Burgundy.

typical_character.aromatics: Citrus, green apple, almond, mineral, white flowers

regional_expressions.style_summary: Neighboring appellation to Gavi, producing Cortese in similar but often fuller style. Less prestigious but good value and gaining recognition.
Broader Piedmont appellation for Cortese. Generally lighter, simpler wines for early drinking. Good everyday value.
Regional designation for Cortese from across Piedmont. Simple, everyday wines.
